title: Rich Italian Lamb Piadina
description: A rich and chewy Italian dish featuring dried mint and a mother sauce base, perfect for a special occasion. This diabetic-friendly dish is made with wholesome ingredients and careful consideration of sugar content.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
Add the lamb shoulder pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the tomato paste, dried mint, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Transfer the Dutch oven to the preheated oven and cook for 2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
While the lamb is cooking, toast the piadina bread in the oven for 5 minutes, or until lightly browned.
Assemble the piadina by spreading a layer of the lamb mixture onto each piece of bread, followed by a sprinkle of crumbled feta cheese.
